title: pittosporum flocculosum
intro: Pittosporum flocculosum is a flowering plant native to New Zealand, also known as Tī Kōuka or Whau. The plant's leaves and bark have been traditionally used in medicine, valued for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Pittosporum flocculosum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tinctures, infusions, and salves, with the bark being used to make a medicinal oil or ointment.
